title: Hieracium schlyteri
taxonomy: Hieracium schlyteri is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other plants in the Asteraceae family, including the presence of composite flowers and a similar leaf structure. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium schlyteri is further classified within the genus Hieracium, a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is divided into several species, with Hieracium schlyteri being a specific species within this group. By classifying Hieracium schlyteri within the Asteraceae family and the genus Hieracium, botanists can better understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Hieracium schlyteri, also known as Schlyter's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium schlyteri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and polyacetylenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Hieracium schlyteri is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used topically to treat skin issues and internally to support immune function.
